arguments_for_no: The amendment was explicitly disabled in rippled v2.6.1 due to a critical bug.
The official status of the amendment is marked as OBSOLETE/removed.
The functionality is intended to be replaced by a future version (PermissionDelegationV1_1), making this specific implementation obsolete.
Activating a known-buggy and obsolete amendment poses unnecessary risk to network stability and validator compatibility.

cited_facts.why_it_matters: Explicitly states the amendment was disabled due to a bug and will be replaced, indicating it should not be voted for.
Confirms the official status is OBSOLETE, meaning it is no longer a valid target for activation.
Highlights the risk of amendment-blocking for incompatible servers, reinforcing the need to avoid buggy/obsolete amendments.

output_json.decision_summary: The PermissionDelegation amendment is obsolete and was disabled due to a bug. It is superseded by a future version. Therefore, the correct governance action is to vote NO to avoid activating a known-defective and deprecated feature.
output_json.forbidden_stronger_claim: The amendment has already been activated on the mainnet.
missing_evidence: Current mainnet activation status (though implied obsolete by F5)
Specific details of the bug in v2.6.1
